bill_or_statute: 2022 AL HB272 / Act 2022-133 / Ala. Code sec. 13A-11-85
coding_notes: HB272 removed the concealed pistol permit requirement for otherwise eligible adults. Open carry was already broadly available with location limits.

bill_or_statute: 2003 AK HB102 / ch. 62 SLA 2003 / Alaska Stat. sec. 11.61.220
coding_notes: Alaska DPS confirms permitless concealed carry for adults 21 and older. Optional permits remain for reciprocity and federal school-zone purposes.

bill_or_statute: 2010 AZ SB1108 / Ariz. Rev. Stat. sec. 13-3102
coding_notes: SB1108 removed the permit requirement for adults 21 and older to carry discreetly. Permits remain relevant for alcohol-serving establishments and reciprocity.

bill_or_statute: 2022 GA SB319 / Georgia Constitutional Carry Act / Ga. Code Ann. sec. 16-11-125.1
coding_notes: SB319 made lawful weapons carriers eligible to carry without a weapons carry license. The governor signed it on April 12 and the state described the effective date as April 12.

bill_or_statute: 2022 IN HB1296 / HEA 1296 / Ind. Code sec. 35-47-2-3
coding_notes: Indiana State Police guidance states that the July 1 2022 law ended the permit requirement to legally carry conceal or transport a handgun for proper persons.

bill_or_statute: 2021 IA HF756 / Iowa Code sec. 724.5
coding_notes: HF756 made carry permits optional and also changed handgun purchase-permit rules. Age coding should be rechecked against Iowa Code before manuscript use.

coding_notes: Missouri veto override occurred in September 2016 and the concealed-carry provision had a January 1 2017 delayed effective date. Local open-carry restrictions can apply to people without permits.
